# The Recruiting Wire — Weekly Auto-Generated Newsletter

**Status:** Design / pending implementation
**Owner:** Taku Noguchi
**Created:** 2026-05-11

## Goal

A weekly newsletter that publishes to a webpage every Friday at 5pm PT, auto-generated by a Claude scheduled agent. Focused on the recruiting industry — especially tech recruiting and AI innovations in recruiting. Read time: ~5 minutes. Skim time: ~2 minutes. URL is shareable; colleagues at the recruiting agency bookmark or visit on demand.

The differentiator vs. a generic news digest is the **synthesis layer**: each issue identifies the pattern of the week and translates it into strategic implications for a tech recruiting agency.

## Audience

Primary: Taku.
Secondary (via URL sharing): colleagues at the recruiting agency — fellow recruiting experts, not novices.

Voice and depth assume an expert peer-to-peer audience. No hype, no LinkedIn-influencer cringe, no explaining what an ATS is.

## Writing rules (mandatory)

The generation prompt **must** load and follow the rules in `ai-writing-rules.md` in the repo. That file is the authoritative style guide for this newsletter — written specifically to eliminate AI writing tells (negative parallelism, "quietly"/"deeply"/"fundamentally" adverbs, "tapestry"/"landscape" nouns, "It's not X — it's Y" reframes, "The X? A Y." rhetorical-question pattern, "serves as" dodge, anaphora abuse, etc.).

Implementation requirements:
- The scheduled routine fetches and includes the contents of `ai-writing-rules.md` in every generation run, so it can't drift away from current rules.
- Before the final draft is written, the routine performs a self-check pass against the rules and rewrites any flagged sentences.
- The preview issue (see "First-run / preview" below) is reviewed specifically for rule violations — if more than 1-2 slip through, the prompt is hardened before the recurring schedule is armed.

## Working Title

"The Recruiting Wire" — placeholder. Replaceable at any time.

## Structure

Every issue follows this section order. **No-padding rule:** sections with fewer than two strong items in a given week are shortened or omitted entirely. Better a tight 4-section issue than a bloated 9-section one.

```
THE RECRUITING WIRE — Week of <Mon date>–<Fri date>

📌 THE WEEK IN ONE PARAGRAPH
2-3 sentences naming the theme / pattern of the week. Pure synthesis,
no items. Reader should know after this paragraph whether to keep
reading or close the page.

🤖 AI IN RECRUITING
2-3 items — tools, launches, model news affecting recruiting.

💼 TECH HIRING & ROLES IN DEMAND
2-3 items — what's hot, who's hiring, role-mix shifts.

📈 FUNDING & HIRING SIGNALS
Companies that closed rounds or hit milestones → likely hiring soon,
with role-area hints when known.

📉 LAYOFFS & TALENT RELEASED
Companies cutting → fresh sourcing pools, with function focus.

💰 COMPENSATION
2-3 items — pay shifts, comp transparency, equity / RSU changes.

🗣️ QUOTES & THREADS WORTH READING
2-3 standout posts from recruiting thought leaders (Josh Bersin,
Hung Lee, Tim Sackett, Madeline Mann, etc.). Quote + 1 line on why
it matters + source link.

⚙️ AI WORKFLOW AUTOMATION IDEAS  (tactical)
1-2 concrete plays the team could try this week. e.g., "Use Claude
+ Greenhouse webhook to auto-draft personalized rejection emails
referencing the specific gap surfaced in the interview kit."

🎯 SO WHAT — STRATEGIC IMPLICATIONS FOR US  (the differentiator)
2-3 short paragraphs answering: given this week's signals, what
should our agency do differently?
Examples of the kind of moves this section surfaces:
  - "Pitch <service line> to clients in segment X who just raised"
  - "Reprice retainers in segment Y given comp shifts"
  - "Build a candidate-side narrative around AI screening fairness
     before clients start asking"
  - "Start sourcing from <laid-off company> this week before the
     rest of the market wakes up to it"

🔗 SOURCES SCANNED THIS WEEK
Brief list — what was pulled from, for transparency.
```

Target length: ~900-1,100 words on a full-content week. Shorter on light weeks (no-padding rule).

The page also has a persistent **[Feedback] button** that opens (or embeds) the feedback form — see the Feedback loop section.

## Two synthesis sections, intentionally

"The Week in One Paragraph" = pattern recognition (what happened).
"So What — Strategic Implications" = recommended action (what we do about it).

These are complementary, not redundant. Top is reader-fast; bottom is the differentiated payload. Both are required sections — they only shrink, never disappear.

## Tactical vs. strategic split

"AI Workflow Automation Ideas" = tactical ("try this play Monday").
"So What — Strategic Implications" = positioning-level ("this changes how we pitch / price / staff").

Different layers, both useful.

## Source policy

The agent draws from three concentric layers, each with a quality/authority filter:

**Trusted backbone (always check):**
- Josh Bersin (joshbersin.com, LinkedIn)
- Hung Lee — Recruiting Brainfood
- ERE / ERE Media
- HR Brew
- SHRM news
- TechCrunch / The Information (tech hiring + HR-tech funding)
- LinkedIn Talent Blog
- WorkforceAI / HR-tech vendor blogs from well-funded players
- Layoffs.fyi (for the Layoffs section)
- Levels.fyi / Pave reports (for Compensation)

**Aggregator layer (run topic queries against):**
Reputable news aggregators that cluster and editorially surface stories. Useful for catching items the trusted backbone missed without descending into open-web noise.
- Google News (topic queries: "AI recruiting", "tech hiring layoffs", "HR tech funding", "tech compensation", "AI hiring lawsuit", etc., past-week filter)
- Techmeme (tech industry signal)
- Hacker News front page (for items tagged hiring / AI / HR-tech with material upvote traction)
- Bloomberg / WSJ / NYT / Reuters tech & labor coverage (for funding, layoffs, and macro hiring stories)
- Crunchbase News / PitchBook News (for funding signals specifically)

**Discovery layer (open web search):**
Open web search for the past 7 days, used to fill gaps the backbone and aggregators don't catch, filtered to:
- Established outlets (excluded: low-traction blogs, content farms, SEO spam)
- Thought leaders with real online traction (followers, peer citations)
- Companies with real market traction (raised material capital, named customers, real product) — not pre-launch vapor

**Excluded:**
- Vendor-paid placements / sponsored posts
- "Top 10 AI recruiting tools" listicles unless from a recognized analyst
- Single-source claims without corroboration on major moves (M&A, layoffs)

## Tech approach

**Architecture:** Static webpage published from a GitHub repo, content auto-generated by an Anthropic scheduled agent.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  GitHub repo  (single source of truth)                      │
│    current-issue.md      ← overwritten each Friday          │
│    learnings.md          ← updated by routine               │
│    feedback/raw/         ← new feedback files appended      │
│    ai-writing-rules.md   ← style guide                      │
│    site/                 ← static HTML/CSS                  │
└────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┬─────────────┘
             │ auto-deploy on push              │ commits via
             ▼                                  │ GitHub API
   ┌─────────────────────┐          ┌───────────┴──────────────┐
   │  GitHub Pages       │          │  Scheduled Claude        │
   │  (public URL)       │          │  routine (Fri 5pm PT)    │
   │  - Latest issue     │          │                          │
   │  - [Feedback] btn   │          └───────────┬──────────────┘
   └──────────┬──────────┘                      │ reads via
              │ link                            │ Drive MCP
              ▼                                 ▲
   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   │  Google Form  →  Google Sheet (feedback log)   │
   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Components:**

1. **GitHub repo** — holds the site, the content, and the feedback archive. Local clone lives at `/Users/takunoguchi/Documents/Recruiting Newsletter Agent/`.
2. **GitHub Pages** — free hosting; auto-deploys on every push to `main`. Public URL, no auth.
3. **Google Form + Google Sheet** — feedback intake. The page's [Feedback] button links to (or embeds) the Form. Submissions become rows in a connected Sheet. **Why a Form, not a custom backend:** zero serverless code to maintain; the routine already has Drive MCP access; submissions are durable and reviewable in the Sheet UI.
4. **Scheduled Claude routine** — created via the `schedule` skill. Runs every Friday at 5pm PT on Anthropic's infrastructure.

**Mechanical flow each Friday:**

1. Routine reads the feedback Sheet via Drive MCP, identifies submissions since the last run.
2. For each new submission, routine commits an MD file into `feedback/raw/YYYY-MM-DD-<short-id>.md` via the GitHub API.
3. Routine reads `learnings.md` plus the new feedback files, extracts durable rules, appends to `learnings.md` (commit).
4. Routine loads `ai-writing-rules.md` + `learnings.md` into the generation prompt.
5. Routine generates the new issue using web search + the source policy.
6. Routine overwrites `current-issue.md` with the new issue (commit).
7. GitHub Pages auto-redeploys on push. New issue is live within ~1 minute.

**Hosting & access:** GitHub Pages, public URL, no password. Initial URL pattern: `https://<github-username>.github.io/recruiting-wire/`. Custom domain can be added later via DNS without rebuilding.

**Open implementation questions (to resolve in the plan):**
- The routine needs a GitHub fine-grained PAT to commit, stored as a routine-level secret. Scope to be worked out in the plan.
- Google Drive MCP must be callable from the scheduled routine context. To verify at setup.
- Sheet rows need a "processed" marker so the same feedback isn't double-imported. Mechanism (a Status column the routine writes, vs. a "last processed timestamp" file in the repo) to be decided in the plan.

## Feedback loop

The newsletter improves over time by capturing feedback submitted via the page's [Feedback] button.

**Where feedback lives:**

- **Intake:** A Google Form linked from the webpage. Submissions land in a connected Google Sheet.
- **Captured feedback:** `feedback/raw/YYYY-MM-DD-<short-id>.md` files in the GitHub repo, one per submission, never edited after creation.
- **Working memory:** `learnings.md` at the repo root — the curated, durable rules the generator follows every run.

**Mechanical flow each Friday at 5pm PT:**

1. Routine reads the feedback Sheet via Drive MCP, identifies submissions since the last run.
2. Each new submission becomes `feedback/raw/YYYY-MM-DD-<short-id>.md` (committed to repo via GitHub API).
3. Routine extracts durable rules from new feedback and appends to `learnings.md` with the date learned (committed).
4. Routine loads `ai-writing-rules.md` + `learnings.md` into the prompt that generates the next issue.

**Why two files (raw + learnings):**

- `raw/` is forever — full audit trail; recovers learnings if pruned wrongly.
- `learnings.md` is curated — small enough to fit every prompt. The routine retires rules that contradict newer feedback (moves them to a "Retired rules" section).

**To give feedback, click the [Feedback] button on the page.** No login required. No special syntax — type what's on your mind.

## Schedule

- **Cadence:** Weekly
- **Time:** Friday 5:00pm America/Los_Angeles
- **Cron:** `0 17 * * 5` in `America/Los_Angeles`
- **Coverage window:** Previous Saturday through generation moment (Friday afternoon)

## First-run / preview

Before the recurring schedule is turned on, generate **one preview issue** so Taku can:
- See the actual output as it will appear on the site
- Adjust section emphasis, tone, layout
- Confirm the source mix produces high-signal items

Options for previewing without making the URL "live to the world":
- Publish to a staging branch / preview deploy.
- Or publish to the main URL with a `noindex` meta tag for the first week, then drop the noindex.

Only after the preview passes the bar does the recurring routine get armed.

## Non-goals

- ❌ Not sending email — page is pull, not push.
- ❌ Not building a multi-issue archive on the site (single-page model; past issues live in the repo's git history, not on the site itself).
- ❌ Not building a candidate-facing product.
- ❌ Not tracking opens, clicks, or engagement metrics.
- ❌ Not building authentication, login, or comments.
- ❌ Not building a custom backend, serverless function, or database.

## Success criteria

After 4 weekly issues, the newsletter is a keeper if:
1. Taku visits the page most weeks.
2. The URL has been shared with at least one colleague (and they've read at least one issue).
3. At least one strategic implication has influenced an actual agency decision (client pitch, sourcing target, pricing).
4. The no-padding rule is holding — issues vary in length based on real signal, not stuffed to a target.

If after 4 weeks none of those are true, scope is wrong and we re-design rather than tune.
